description: In 2008, nearly 300,000 infants in China were sickened by milk powder tainted with melamine, resulting in several deaths. The event caused a collapse in trust for domestic dairy brands and led to a massive government crackdown on food production standards.

key_events.description: Sanlu Group recalls tainted formula after initial denial.
Official government admission of systemic melamine contamination.
Public protests against dairy conglomerates erupt.
Execution of individuals responsible for adulteration.

calibration_notes: Models extreme erosion of public trust in corporate/government oversight when high-severity safety failures occur.
